features.description: Define SessionMetadata interface and SessionState discriminated union for session detection
steps: Then it has fields: sessionId (string), specPath (string), startedAt (string), pid (number)
Then it is a discriminated union with variants: { status: 'inactive' }, { status: 'active', pid: number, metadata: SessionMetadata }, { status: 'stale', pid: number }

features.description: Implement PID file and session metadata file path helpers in session-state.ts
Implement readPidFile() that reads and parses the PID integer from ~/.harness/current.pid
Implement isProcessAlive() that uses kill -0 to check if a PID is still running
steps: Given a PID that corresponds to a running process
Given a PID that does not correspond to any running process
Given process.kill throws EPERM (process exists but permission denied)
Then it returns true (process is alive, just not owned by us)

features.description: Implement readSessionMetadata() that reads and parses ~/.harness/current-session.json
steps: Given ~/.harness/current-session.json contains valid JSON with sessionId, specPath, startedAt, pid
Then it returns a SessionMetadata object with all fields populated
Then it returns null without throwing

features.description: Implement cleanupStaleFiles() that removes ~/.harness/current.pid and current-session.json
steps: Then both files are removed from disk
Then the PID file is removed and no error is thrown for the missing session file

features.description: Implement getSessionState() that orchestrates PID file check, liveness test, and metadata read
steps: Given ~/.harness/current.pid exists with a live PID and current-session.json is present
Then it returns { status: 'active', pid, metadata } with all fields
Given ~/.harness/current.pid exists but the PID process is dead
Then it returns { status: 'stale', pid } and calls cleanupStaleFiles()
Given ~/.harness/current.pid exists with a live PID but current-session.json is missing
Then it returns { status: 'active', pid, metadata: null }

features.description: Implement writePidFile() and writeSessionMetadata() for harness run startup lifecycle
steps: Then ~/.harness/current.pid is created containing the PID as a string
Then ~/.harness/current-session.json is created with the JSON-serialized metadata
Given ~/.harness/ directory does not yet exist
When writePidFile() or writeSessionMetadata() is called
Then the directory is created recursively before writing

features.description: Implement formatElapsed() that converts an ISO startedAt timestamp to a human-readable elapsed string
steps: Given a startedAt timestamp that is exactly 23 minutes and 15 seconds in the past
Given a startedAt timestamp that is 1 hour and 2 minutes in the past
Given a startedAt timestamp that is 45 seconds in the past
Given a startedAt timestamp that is in the future or invalid

features.description: Implement deriveSpecName() that extracts a short spec name from a full specPath
steps: Then it returns 'mini-jwt' (the parent directory name)
Given specPath is an empty string

features.description: Implement statusCommand() output for the no-session-active path
steps: Then stdout contains 'No active harness session'
And stdout contains 'harness run <spec.md>' as guidance
And the process exits with code 0

features.description: Implement statusCommand() output for the active session path with all display fields
steps: Given ~/.harness/current.pid contains a live PID and current-session.json has valid metadata
And stdout contains the sessionId value
And stdout contains the spec name derived from specPath
And stdout contains the formatted elapsed time
And stdout contains the PID value

features.description: Implement statusCommand() stale session detection: cleans up files and prints no-session message
steps: Given ~/.harness/current.pid exists but the PID process is dead
And stdout contains 'No active harness session'
And the process exits with code 0

features.description: Implement statusCommand() graceful fallback when session JSON is missing despite live PID
steps: Given ~/.harness/current.pid contains a live PID but current-session.json does not exist
Then stdout contains 'RUNNING' and the PID
And missing fields (sessionId, spec, elapsed) are shown as 'unknown' or omitted
And the process does not crash or exit with a non-zero code

features.description: Add 'status' command to help text in src/index.tsx
steps: Then the output contains a 'status' entry under Commands
And the description mentions showing whether a harness process is running

features.description: Add 'status' case to routeCommand() switch in src/index.tsx and wire up import
steps: Given src/index.tsx has a routeCommand() switch statement
When routeCommand() is called with cli.input = ['status']
When routeCommand() is called with cli.input = ['unknownCmd']
Then statusCommand() is NOT called and help is shown
Given statusCommand is imported from src/commands/status.tsx
